1316	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (gluing step,
1317	  Algebra Lemma \texttt{lemma-cover}).}
1318	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(X\), and
1319	  \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) is a finite
1320	  family with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) (so the basic opens \(D(g)\),
1321	  \(g \in t\), cover \(W\)), and that for each \(g \in t\) the section module
1322	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\). Then \(\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1323	  is a finite \(\Gamma(X, W)\)-module.
1324	\end{lemma}
1325	
1326	\begin{proof}
1327	  \uses{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen, lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer,
1328	        lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
1329	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (gluing step).}
1330	  By the local-on-a-spanning-family criterion \cref{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan}
1331	  it suffices to show, for each \(g \in t\), that the localization of
1332	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) at \(g\) is finite over the localization
1333	  \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\). Fix \(g \in t\). The basic open \(D(g)\) is affine, and the
1334	  restriction \(\Gamma(X, W) \to \Gamma(X, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(g))\) as
1335	  the away-localization \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\)
1336	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); moreover the section restriction
1337	  \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D(g))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) as the
1338	  localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\)
1339	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}). Thus \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is a
1340	  model of the localized module, finite over \(\Gamma(X, D(g)) = (\Gamma(X, W))_g\)

1342	  \cref{lem:finite_localizedModule_transfer} carries this finiteness to the

1344	  \((\Gamma(X, W))_g\). This supplies the per-element hypothesis, and
1345	  \cref{lem:module_finite_of_localizationSpan} concludes that \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) is
1346	  finite over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).

1349	\subsection{Transport engine for generating sections}
1350	
1351	The restriction-of-generators lemma below is assembled from a generic transport
1352	principle: a finite generating family of a sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{M}\) pushes
1353	forward to a finite generating family of \(F(\mathcal{M})\) along any functor \(F\) of
1354	sheaves of modules that preserves colimits and carries the structure sheaf to the
1355	structure sheaf. The three blocks of this subsection package that principle (the
1356	generating family, its index type, and the finiteness bookkeeping). They are
1357	project-bespoke: Mathlib provides the analogous transport only for full presentations
1358	(with kernel data), not for bare generating families.
1359	
1360	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1361	[Transport of a generating family along a colimit-preserving functor]
1362	  \label{lem:genSections_map}
1363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map}
1364	  Let \(F : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to \mathrm{Mod}\,S\) be a functor of sheaves of modules
1365	  that preserves colimits and is equipped with an isomorphism
1366	  \(\eta : F(\OO_R) \xrightarrow{\sim} \OO_S\) carrying the unit (structure sheaf) of the
1367	  source to that of the target. Given a generating family \(\sigma\) of \(\mathcal{M}\)
1368	  with index set \(I = \sigma.I\) and epimorphism
1369	  \(\sigma.\pi : \OO_R^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{M}\), the images
1370	  \((\mathrm{mapFree}\,F\,\eta\,I)^{-1} \circ F(\sigma.\pi)\) form a generating family of
1371	  \(F(\mathcal{M})\) with the same index set \(I\). The map is an epimorphism because it
1372	  is the composite of an isomorphism with \(F(\sigma.\pi)\), and \(F\) preserves the
1373	  epimorphism \(\sigma.\pi\).
1374	\end{lemma}
1375	
1376	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1377	[The transported family keeps its index type]
1378	  \label{lem:genSections_map_I}
1379	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map_I}
1380	  \uses{lem:genSections_map}
1381	  The index set of the transported generating family of \cref{lem:genSections_map} equals
1382	  the index set of the original: \((\mathrm{map}\,\sigma\,F\,\eta)\!.I = \sigma.I\). This
1383	  holds definitionally.
1384	\end{lemma}
1385	
1386	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1387	[Finiteness survives transport]
1388	  \label{lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.map_isFiniteType}
1390	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I}
1391	  If the original generating family \(\sigma\) is of finite type (its index set is finite),
1392	  then so is the transported family \(\mathrm{map}\,\sigma\,F\,\eta\): the index type is
1393	  unchanged by \cref{lem:genSections_map_I}, hence still finite.
1394	\end{lemma}
1395	
1396	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1397	[Half 2, seam 1a — local generators restrict to a smaller open]
1398	  \label{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1399	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localGenerators_restrict}
1400	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I, lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1401	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\F\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
1402	  Suppose that on an open \(Y \subseteq X\) the restriction \(\F|_Y\) is
1403	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Then for any open \(V \le Y\) the
1404	  restricted family — the images of those generators under the section-restriction
1405	  \(\Gamma(\F, Y) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) — is again a finite generating family of
1406	  \(\F|_V\). Generation survives passage to the smaller open (a family that generates
1407	  every stalk over \(Y\) generates every stalk over \(V\)), and the index set, and hence
1408	  finiteness, is unchanged.
1409	\end{lemma}
1410	
1411	\begin{proof}
1412	  \uses{lem:genSections_map, lem:genSections_map_I, lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}
1413	  A family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_Y\) precisely when the
1414	  induced map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) is surjective on every stalk
1415	  \(\F_y\,(y \in Y)\). Restricting along the inclusion \(V \le Y\) the
1416	  generators map to \(\{s_i|_V\}_{i \in I}\), and for a point \(x \in V\) the stalk
1417	  \(\F_x\) and the comparison map agree with the value at \(x\) computed over \(Y\), since
1418	  stalks are insensitive to shrinking the ambient open. Hence the induced map
1419	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus I} \to \F|_V\) is surjective on every stalk over \(V\), so
1420	  \(\{s_i|_V\}_{i \in I}\) generates \(\F|_V\). The index set \(I\) is the same
1421	  finite set, so finiteness is preserved. Concretely the restricted family is obtained by
1422	  transporting the generating family along the (colimit-preserving, unit-preserving)
1423	  restriction functors via the transport engine \cref{lem:genSections_map}, whose index
1424	  type is unchanged (\cref{lem:genSections_map_I}) and which preserves finiteness
1425	  (\cref{lem:genSections_map_isFiniteType}).
1426	\end{proof}
1427	
1428	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1429	[Half 2, seam 1b — finite standard-basic-open subcover of an affine open]
1430	  \label{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1431	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover}
1432	  Let \(W\) be an affine open of a scheme \(X\), with the canonical identification
1433	  \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\). Given an arbitrary open cover \(\{U_\alpha\}\) of \(W\),
1434	  there is a finite family \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1435	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that each basic open \(D(g)\) (\(g \in t\)) is
1436	  contained in some cover member \(U_\alpha\). In particular the \(D(g)\,(g \in t)\) form a
1437	  finite standard-basic-open subcover of \(W\) refining \(\{U_\alpha\}\).
1438	\end{lemma}
1439	
1440	\begin{proof}
1441	  Under \(W \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) the basic opens \(D(g)\,(g \in \Gamma(X, W))\) form a
1442	  basis of the topology (\(\mathrm{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens}\)). Hence each
1443	  cover member \(U_\alpha\) is a union of basic opens, and the collection of all basic
1444	  opens \(D(g)\) that are contained in some \(U_\alpha\) still covers \(W\). The affine
1445	  scheme \(W\) is quasi-compact, so finitely many of these basic opens
1446	  \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already cover \(W\); set
1447	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\), a finite family, each \(D(g_i)\)
1448	  contained in some \(U_\alpha\) by construction. That the \(D(g_i)\) cover
1449	  \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) — i.e. no prime ideal contains all the \(g_i\) — is exactly
1450	  the condition \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\).
1451	\end{proof}

1453	\begin{lemma}[Half 2, seam 1c — finite generation equals a finite free epimorphism]
1454	  \label{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1456	  Let \(Y\) be an open of a scheme and \(\F\) a sheaf of modules on \(Y\). Then
1457	  \(\F\) is globally generated by a finite family of sections if and only if there is a
1458	  finite index set \(I\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_Y\)-modules
1459	  \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F\). No quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is assumed:
1460	  the statement holds for arbitrary sheaves of modules on \(Y\), and applies in particular
1461	  to the restrictions \(\F|_{D(g)}\) arising in the cover assembly.
1462	\end{lemma}
1463	
1464	\begin{proof}
1465	  A finite family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(\F|_Y\) is the same datum as an
1466	  \(\OO_Y\)-linear map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) sending the \(i\)-th basis section to
1467	  \(s_i\). The family generates \(\F|_Y\) (the induced map is surjective on every stalk)
1468	  exactly when this map is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Thus a finite generating
1469	  family yields a finite-free epimorphism, and conversely the images of the basis sections
1470	  of any epimorphism \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_Y\) with \(I\) finite form a
1471	  finite generating family.
1472	\end{proof}
1473	
1474	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1475	[Half 2, seam 1 — finite type yields a finite cover of globally-generated affines]
1476	  \label{lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated}
1478	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1479	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2101-L2107).
1480	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1481	  % "Assume $\widetilde M$ is a finite type $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. This means
1482	  %  there exists an open covering of $X$ such that $\widetilde M$ restricted to
1483	  %  the members of this covering is globally generated by finitely many sections.
1484	  %  Thus there also exists a standard open covering
1485	  %  $X = \bigcup_{i = 1, \ldots, n} D(f_i)$ such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$
1486	  %  is generated by finitely many sections."
1487	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1488	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1489	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (standard
1490	  cover of generated affines).}
1491	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F\) a sheaf of modules of finite type,
1492	  and \(W \subseteq X\) an affine open. Then there is a finite family
1493	  \(t \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) such that for
1494	  each \(g \in t\) the basic open \(D(g)\) is affine and \(\F|_{D(g)}\) is globally
1495	  generated by finitely many sections; equivalently, for each \(g \in t\) there is
1496	  a finite index set \(I_g\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g)}\)-modules
1497	  \(\OO_{D(g)}^{\oplus I_g} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g)}\).
1498	\end{lemma}
1499	
1500	\begin{proof}
1501	  \uses{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict, lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover,
1502	        lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}
1503	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (standard cover of generated affines).}
1504	  Finite type of \(\F\) means that \(X\) has an open covering \(\{Y_\alpha\}\) on each
1505	  member of which \(\F\) carries a finite generating family of sections. The intersections
1506	  \(\{Y_\alpha \cap W\}\) form an open cover of the affine \(W\). By the finite standard
1507	  subcover refinement \cref{lem:gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover} there is a finite family
1508	  \(t = \{g_1, \dots, g_n\} \subseteq \Gamma(X, W)\) with
1509	  \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\) and each basic open \(D(g_i)\) contained in some
1510	  \(Y_\alpha \cap W\) — in particular \(D(g_i) \subseteq W\) is affine, and \(D(g_i)\)
1511	  embeds by an open immersion into the member \(Y_\alpha\) on which \(\F\) is finitely
1512	  generated. By the restriction-of-generators lemma
1513	  \cref{lem:gf_localGenerators_restrict} the finite generating family on \(Y_\alpha\)
1514	  restricts to a finite generating family of \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\), so \(\F|_{D(g_i)}\) is
1515	  globally generated by finitely many sections. Finally, by the free-epimorphism
1516	  reformulation \cref{lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi}, this is equivalent to the existence
1517	  of a finite index set \(I_{g_i}\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_{D(g_i)}\)-modules
1518	  \(\OO_{D(g_i)}^{\oplus I_{g_i}} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_{D(g_i)}\). That the \(D(g_i)\)
1519	  cover \(W = \Spec \Gamma(X, W)\) is exactly \(\mathrm{Ideal.span}\,t = \top\). This is
1520	  Stacks' passage from the local-generation datum to a standard finite cover of generated
1521	  affines.
1522	\end{proof}

1524	\begin{lemma}[Affine $\widetilde{(-)}\dashv\Gamma$ adjunction with its $\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ counit]
1525	  \label{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib}
1526	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction}
1527	  \mathlibok
1528	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}).}
1529	  On the affine scheme \(\Spec R\) the associated-sheaf functor
1530	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}\,R \to (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules}\) is left adjoint to the
1531	  affine section functor \(\Gamma : (\Spec R).\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{Mod}\,R\) (Mathlib's
1532	  \(\mathrm{moduleSpecΓFunctor}\), evaluation of a sheaf of modules at the top open). The counit of
1533	  this adjunction is the natural transformation
1534	  \(\Gamma(-)\,\widetilde{\phantom{x}} \to \mathrm{id}\) whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\)
1535	  is \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, \top)} \to \mathcal{M}\), and
1536	  the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful.
1537	\end{lemma}

1539	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1540	[Half 2, seam 2 — $\Gamma$ on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections]
1541	  \label{lem:gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1542	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi}
1543	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1544	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1545	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB, proof
1546	  % (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2106-L2107) — the implication
1547	  % "$\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ generated by finitely many sections $\Rightarrow$
1548	  % $M_{f_i}$ finitely generated" is exactly section-level surjectivity of a sheaf
1549	  % epi over an affine.
1550	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
1551	  % "such that $\widetilde M|_{D(f_i)}$ is generated by finitely many sections.
1552	  %  Thus $M_{f_i}$ is finitely generated for each $i$."
1553	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", Tag 01PB (sections of a
1554	  generated sheaf over an affine).}
1555	  Let \(V\) be an affine open of a scheme, with \(B = \Gamma(X, V)\) and
1556	  \(V \cong \Spec B\), and let \(\pi : \mathcal{G} \to \F\) be an epimorphism of
1557	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules over \(V\). Then the induced map on sections
1558	  \(\Gamma(\pi) : \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a surjective
1559	  \(B\)-module map.
1560	\end{lemma}
1561	
1562	\begin{proof}
1563	  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib,
1564	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
1565	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01PB (sections of a generated sheaf over an
1566	  affine).}
1567	  Write \(V \cong \Spec B\) and let \(\Gamma\) be the affine section functor on
1568	  \((\Spec B).\mathrm{Modules}\), so that \(\Gamma(\pi) = \Gamma(\mathcal{G}, V) \to
1569	  \Gamma(\F, V)\) is the map in question. By \cref{lem:tilde_adjunction_mathlib} the
1570	  associated-sheaf functor \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left adjoint to \(\Gamma\), with counit the
1571	  natural transformation whose component at a sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is
1572	  \(\mathcal{M}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{M}, V)} \to \mathcal{M}\).
1573	
1574	  Because \(\mathcal{G}\) and \(\F\) are quasi-coherent, the qcoh-sections descent
1575	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}, fed through the counit-iso assembly
1576	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}) makes both counit components
1577	  \(\mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) and \(\F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) isomorphisms. Naturality of the
1578	  counit on \(\pi\) gives the commuting square
1579	  \[
1580	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)} \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1}
1581	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi,
1582	    \qquad\text{equivalently}\qquad
1583	    \widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}
1584	      = \F.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}^{-1} \circ \pi \circ \mathcal{G}.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}.
1585	  \]
1586	  The right-hand side is a composite of an isomorphism, the epimorphism \(\pi\), and an
1587	  isomorphism, hence is itself an epimorphism. Thus \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\pi)}\) is an epimorphism
1588	  of sheaves of modules. Since the left adjoint \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is faithful, it reflects
1589	  epimorphisms; therefore \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathrm{Mod}\,B\), i.e. the
1590	  \(B\)-linear map \(\Gamma(\pi)\) is surjective.
1591	
1592	  This is the structural content of the vanishing of higher quasi-coherent cohomology on an
1593	  affine — the failure of \(\Gamma\) to be right exact would obstruct exactly this surjectivity —
1594	  obtained here directly from the affine adjunction without invoking a cohomology computation.
1595	\end{proof}
1596

1668	\begin{lemma}
1669	
1670	\leanok
1671	[Finiteness transports across a ring isomorphism and a semilinear additive map]
1672	  \label{lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1673	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1674	  Let \(\sigma : R \xrightarrow{\sim} R'\) be a ring isomorphism and
1675	  \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) an additive isomorphism that is \(\sigma\)-semilinear,
1676	  i.e. \(e(r \cdot m) = \sigma(r) \cdot e(m)\) for all \(r \in R\), \(m \in M\). If
1677	  \(M\) is a finite \(R\)-module then \(M'\) is a finite \(R'\)-module.
1678	\end{lemma}
1679	
1680	\begin{proof}
1681	  Pick a finite \(R\)-spanning set \(T \subseteq M\). The image \(e(T)\) is finite, and
1682	  it \(R'\)-spans \(M'\): for \(m' \in M'\) write \(m' = e(m)\) with
1683	  \(m = \sum_i r_i t_i\) (\(t_i \in T\)); then
1684	  \(m' = e(\sum_i r_i t_i) = \sum_i \sigma(r_i)\, e(t_i)\) by semilinearity, an
1685	  \(R'\)-combination of \(e(T)\). Hence the top submodule of \(M'\) is finitely
1686	  generated, i.e. \(M'\) is a finite \(R'\)-module.
1687	\end{proof}

1689	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1690	[G1 base case — finite affine sections from a finite generating family]
1691	  \label{lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1692	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_genSections}
1693	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1694	        lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear}
1695	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\F : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules, and
1696	  \(D \subseteq X\) an affine open. Suppose the restriction
1697	  \(\F|_D = (\mathrm{pullback}\,D.\iota)^{*}\F\) carries a finite generating family
1698	  \(\sigma\), with index set \(I = \sigma.I\). Then the section module
1699	  \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) is a finite module over \(\Gamma(X, D)\). This is the affine base
1700	  case of \(G1\): once the generators are in hand on an affine open, finiteness of
1701	  sections is purely an affine (Spec-side) statement. (Only quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is
1702	  needed — the finite-generation datum is carried by \(\sigma\) directly, so no separate
1703	  finite-type hypothesis on \(\F\) is required.)
1704	\end{lemma}
1705	
1706	\begin{proof}
1707	  \uses{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi, lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ,
1708	        lem:module_finite_of_ringEquiv_semilinear, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
1709	  The content is the transport of the finite generating datum
1710	  from the category of sheaves of modules over the affine open \(D\) to the affine
1711	  scheme \(\Spec \Gamma(X, D)\), where the affine base case applies. Write
1712	  \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\) and use the canonical identification
1713	  \(D \cong \Spec R\) (\(\mathrm{isoSpec}\)). The argument has three steps.
1714	
1715	  \emph{(a) Quasi-coherence on \(\Spec R\).} Transport \(\F|_D\) along
1716	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) to a sheaf of modules \(\F'\) on \(\Spec R\). Finite type of
1717	  \(\F\) is inherited by the restriction, and quasi-coherence of \(\F'\) is the assertion
1718	  that its counit \(\F'.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism; this is the affine
1719	  quasi-coherence criterion \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ} fed by the basic-open
1720	  localization datum \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}.
1721	
1722	  \emph{(b) A finite free epimorphism over \(\Spec R\).} The generating family \(\sigma\)
1723	  furnishes an epimorphism \(\OO_D^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_D\). Transporting along
1724	  \(\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1}\) and using that the pullback of the free module along an
1725	  isomorphism is again free (\((\mathrm{pullback}\,\mathrm{isoSpec}^{-1})^{*}\OO_D^{\oplus I}
1726	  \cong \OO_{\Spec R}^{\oplus I}\)), together with the identification of the free sheaf with
1727	  the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\) of \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) (the associated-sheaf
1728	  functor preserves coproducts and \(\widetilde{R} \cong \OO_{\Spec R}\)), gives an
1729	  epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) with \(N = R^{\oplus I}\) a finite
1730	  \(R\)-module (\(I\) finite).
1731	
1732	  \emph{(c) Identification of section modules.} The affine global-sections functor sends
1733	  \(\F'\) to \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\), which the transport identifies with \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1734	  as \(R = \Gamma(X, D)\)-modules.
1735	
1736	  Now the affine base case \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi}, applied to the finite-free
1737	  quasi-coherent epimorphism \(\widetilde{N} \twoheadrightarrow \F'\) over \(\Spec R\),
1738	  shows \(\Gamma(\F', \top)\) is a finite \(R\)-module; by (c) this is \(\Gamma(\F, D)\)
1739	  finite over \(\Gamma(X, D)\).
1740	\end{proof}

1782	  finiteness statements to give \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) finite over \(\Gamma(X, W)\).

2819	/-- **Flatness transfers across a ring isomorphism with a compatible (semilinear) additive
2820	isomorphism.**  If `e : R ≃+* R'` is a ring iso, `l : M ≃+ M'` an additive iso intertwining the
2821	scalar actions (`l (r • x) = e r • l x`), and `M` is flat over `R`, then `M'` is flat over `R'`.
2822	The two canonical localization models `LocalizedModule (powers f) M` (over `Localization.Away f`)
2823	and a geometric `Γ(F, D f)` (over `Γ(S, D f)`) are related precisely this way, so this is the
2824	single transfer between canonical-model freeness and the geometric base ring.  Proof: `M'` becomes
2825	the base change `R' ⊗[R] M` along `e` (the lift of `l` is an `R'`-linear equiv, inverse
2826	`y ↦ 1 ⊗ l⁻¹ y`), so flatness descends by `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`. -/
2827	theorem flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear {R R' M M' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R']
2828	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M']
2829	    (e : R ≃+* R') (l : M ≃+ M')
2830	    (hl : ∀ (r : R) (x : M), l (r • x) = e r • l x) [Module.Flat R M] :
2831	    Module.Flat R' M' := by
2832	  letI algRR' : Algebra R R' := (e : R →+* R').toAlgebra
2833	  letI modRM' : Module R M' := Module.compHom M' (e : R →+* R')
2834	  haveI towerRR'M' : IsScalarTower R R' M' :=
2835	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2836	  -- `l` upgraded to `R`-linear (the `R`-action on `M'` is restriction along `e`).
2837	  let lR : M →ₗ[R] M' :=
2838	    { toFun := l, map_add' := l.map_add,
2839	      map_smul' := fun r x => by show l (r • x) = e r • l x; rw [hl] }
2840	  -- semilinearity of the inverse, used to make `y ↦ 1 ⊗ l⁻¹ y` an `R'`-linear inverse.
2841	  have hsymm : ∀ (r' : R') (y : M'), l.symm (r' • y) = e.symm r' • l.symm y := by
2842	    intro r' y
2843	    apply l.injective
2844	    rw [hl, e.apply_symm_apply, l.apply_symm_apply, l.apply_symm_apply]
2845	  -- `s •_R (1 ⊗ m) = (e s) ⊗ m` (the `R`-action on the base change is via `e`).
2846	  have hkey : ∀ (s : R) (m : M),
2847	      s • ((1 : R') ⊗ₜ[R] m) = (e s) ⊗ₜ[R] m := by
2848	    intro s m
2849	    rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', Algebra.smul_def, mul_one]
2850	    rfl
2851	  let jfun : M' →ₗ[R'] TensorProduct R R' M :=
2852	    { toFun := fun y => (1 : R') ⊗ₜ[R] l.symm y,
2853	      map_add' := fun y y' => by rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add],
2854	      map_smul' := fun r' y => by
2855	        show (1 : R') ⊗ₜ[R] l.symm (r' • y) = r' • ((1 : R') ⊗ₜ[R] l.symm y)
2856	        rw [hsymm, TensorProduct.tmul_smul, hkey, e.apply_symm_apply,
2857	          TensorProduct.smul_tmul', smul_eq_mul, mul_one] }
2858	  have hbc : IsBaseChange R' lR :=
2859	    IsBaseChange.of_equiv
2860	      (LinearEquiv.ofLinear (lR.liftBaseChange R') jfun
2861	        (by ext y; simp [jfun, lR, LinearMap.liftBaseChange_tmul])
2862	        (by
2863	          apply LinearMap.ext
2864	          intro z
2865	          induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
2866	          | zero => simp
2867	          | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq] at ha hb ⊢; rw [ha, hb]
2868	          | tmul r' m =>
2869	            show jfun (lR.liftBaseChange R' (r' ⊗ₜ[R] m)) = r' ⊗ₜ[R] m
2870	            rw [LinearMap.liftBaseChange_tmul]
2871	            show (1 : R') ⊗ₜ[R] l.symm (r' • lR m) = r' ⊗ₜ[R] m
2872	            have hlm : l.symm (r' • lR m) = e.symm r' • m := by
2873	              rw [hsymm]; congr 1; exact l.symm_apply_apply m
2874	            rw [hlm, TensorProduct.tmul_smul, hkey, e.apply_symm_apply]))
2875	      (fun x => by simp [lR, LinearMap.liftBaseChange_tmul])
2876	  exact Module.Flat.isBaseChange (R := R) (M := M) (S := R') (N := M') (f := lR) hbc

2878	/-- **Flatness of a localization is independent of the chosen localization models.**  If `Mₛ` and
2879	`Mₛ'` are both localizations of the `A`-module `M` at `S` (over the respective localization rings
2880	`Rₛ`, `Rₛ'` of `A` at `S`), then `Mₛ` flat over `Rₛ` implies `Mₛ'` flat over `Rₛ'`.  This is the
2881	bridge between the canonical-model freeness (`LocalizedModule (powers f) M` over `Localization.Away
2882	f`) and the geometric model (`Γ(F, D f)` over `Γ(S, D f)`).  Proof: the localization-uniqueness
2883	ring iso `e := IsLocalization.algEquiv` and module iso `l := IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` are
2884	semilinear (cleared by acting by a denominator `s ∈ S` and cancelling), so `flat_of_ringEquiv_-
2885	semilinear` applies. -/
2886	theorem flat_localization_models {A M : Type*} [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M]
2887	    (S : Submonoid A)
2888	    {Rₛ : Type*} [CommRing Rₛ] [Algebra A Rₛ] [IsLocalization S Rₛ]
2889	    {Mₛ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₛ] [Module A Mₛ] [Module Rₛ Mₛ] [IsScalarTower A Rₛ Mₛ]
2890	    (g : M →ₗ[A] Mₛ) [IsLocalizedModule S g]
2891	    {Rₛ' : Type*} [CommRing Rₛ'] [Algebra A Rₛ'] [IsLocalization S Rₛ']
2892	    {Mₛ' : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₛ'] [Module A Mₛ'] [Module Rₛ' Mₛ'] [IsScalarTower A Rₛ' Mₛ']
2893	    (g' : M →ₗ[A] Mₛ') [IsLocalizedModule S g'] [Module.Flat Rₛ Mₛ] :
2894	    Module.Flat Rₛ' Mₛ' := by
2895	  let ea : Rₛ ≃ₐ[A] Rₛ' := IsLocalization.algEquiv S Rₛ Rₛ'
2896	  let e : Rₛ ≃+* Rₛ' := ea.toRingEquiv
2897	  let l : Mₛ ≃ₗ[A] Mₛ' := IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S g g'
2898	  refine flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear e l.toAddEquiv ?_
2899	  intro c x
2900	  change l (c • x) = e c • l x
2901	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hrs⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S c
2902	  have hsc : (s : A) • c = algebraMap A Rₛ a := by
2903	    rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← hrs, mul_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_spec]
2904	  have hsec : (s : A) • e c = algebraMap A Rₛ' a := by
2905	    have hsm : (s : A) • e c = ea ((s : A) • c) := (map_smul ea (s : A) c).symm
2906	    rw [hsm, hsc, ea.commutes]
2907	  have hunit : IsUnit ((algebraMap A (Module.End A Mₛ')) (s : A)) :=
2908	    IsLocalizedModule.map_units g' s
2909	  have hbij := (Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hunit
2910	  apply hbij.injective
2911	  change (algebraMap A (Module.End A Mₛ') (s : A)) (l (c • x))
2912	       = (algebraMap A (Module.End A Mₛ') (s : A)) (e c • l x)
2913	  simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
2914	  have hL : (s : A) • (c • x) = (a : A) • x := by
2915	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rₛ (s : A) (c • x), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def, hsc,
2916	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2917	  have hR : (s : A) • (e c • l x) = (a : A) • l x := by
2918	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rₛ' (s : A) (e c • l x), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def, hsec,
2919	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2920	  rw [← map_smul l (s : A) (c • x), hL, hR, map_smul l]

2927	image of `f`) as an `A`-localization of `Γ(F, W)` at `powers f`. -/

2929	    {A B M N : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
2930	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module B M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
2931	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower A B N]
2932	    (f : A) (φ : M →ₗ[B] N)
2933	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap A B f)) φ] :
2934	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (φ.restrictScalars A) where
2935	  map_units s := by
2936	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
2937	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
2938	    have hmem : (algebraMap A B f) ^ k ∈ Submonoid.powers (algebraMap A B f) := ⟨k, rfl⟩
2939	    have hB : IsUnit (algebraMap B (Module.End B N) ((algebraMap A B f) ^ k)) :=
2940	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units φ ⟨(algebraMap A B f) ^ k, hmem⟩
2941	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hB
2942	    have hfun : ⇑(algebraMap A (Module.End A N) (s : A))
2943	        = ⇑(algebraMap B (Module.End B N) ((algebraMap A B f) ^ k)) := by
2944	      funext n
2945	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, ← hk, ← map_pow,
2946	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2947	    rw [hfun]; exact hB

2950	      IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap A B f)) φ y
2951	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := t.2
2952	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
2953	    show (f ^ k : A) • y = φ x
2954	    have hsm : (f ^ k : A) • y = (t : B) • y := by
2955	      rw [← hk]
2956	      show (f ^ k : A) • y = (algebraMap A B f) ^ k • y
2957	      rw [← map_pow, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2958	    rw [hsm]; exact e
2959	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} h := by
2960	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
2961	      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap A B f)) (f := φ) h
2962	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := c.2
2963	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ k, k, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
2964	    have hsm : ∀ m : M, (f ^ k : A) • m = (c : B) • m := by
2965	      intro m
2966	      rw [← hk]
2967	      show (f ^ k : A) • m = (algebraMap A B f) ^ k • m
2968	      rw [← map_pow, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]

2970	    rw [hsm, hsm]; exact hc

2973	
2974	The four lemmas `gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq` (B2.1), `gf_section_localization_twoleg`
2975	(B2.2), `gf_base_localization_comparison` (B2.3), `gf_crossChart_spanning_cover`

2979	/-- **B2.1 — cross-chart basic-open identity** (`lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq`).
2980	Two sections `g ∈ Γ(X, W)`, `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wⱼ)` on overlapping opens that restrict to the
2981	*same* section over the overlap `O = W ⊓ Wⱼ`, and whose basic opens both lie inside `O`,
2982	cut out the *same* open `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`. Pure geometry over
2983	`Scheme.basicOpen_res`: `X.basicOpen (g|_O) = O ⊓ X.basicOpen g`, which collapses to
2984	`X.basicOpen g` since `X.basicOpen g ≤ O`; the matching hypothesis equates the two
2985	left-hand sides. Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2986	theorem gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq {X : Scheme.{u}} {W Wj : X.Opens}
2987	    (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj))
2988	    (hg : X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wj) (hgbar : X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wj)
2989	    (hmatch : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g
2990	            = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar) :
2991	    X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2992	  have h1 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g)
2993	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen g := X.basicOpen_res g _
2994	  have h2 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar)
2995	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen gbar := X.basicOpen_res gbar _
2996	  rw [hmatch, h2, inf_eq_right.mpr hgbar] at h1
2997	  rw [inf_eq_right.mpr hg] at h1
2998	  exact h1.symm

1929	  instantiated with the canonical localizations \(N_{\mathfrak q}\) of \(N\) at the
1930	  maximal ideals \(\mathfrak q\) of \(B\): each \(N_{\mathfrak q}\) is a localized
1931	  module of \(N\) at the prime \(\mathfrak q\) of the source ring \(B\), and the
1932	  hypothesis supplies the required flatness of every \(N_{\mathfrak q}\) over \(R\).

1940	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1941	[B1.0 — localization commutes with base tensor]
1942	  \label{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1943	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1944	  Let \(R \to B\) be a ring map, \(T \subseteq B\) a submonoid, \(N\) a \(B\)-module,
1945	  and \(K\) an \(R\)-module. There is an isomorphism, natural in \(K\),
1946	  \[
1947	    \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,(N \otimes_R K)
1948	      \;\cong\; (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K .
1949	  \]
1950	\end{lemma}
1951	\begin{proof}
1952	  Localization is base change: \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,M \cong T^{-1}B \otimes_B M\)
1953	  naturally in the \(B\)-module \(M\). Applying this to \(M = N \otimes_R K\) and using
1954	  associativity/commutativity of the tensor product over the tower
1955	  \(R \to B \to T^{-1}B\) gives
1956	  \(T^{-1}B \otimes_B (N \otimes_R K) \cong (T^{-1}B \otimes_B N) \otimes_R K
1957	  \cong (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K\); naturality in \(K\) is inherited
1958	  from the tensor functor.
1959	\end{proof}

1961	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1962	[B1 — localizing the source ring preserves flatness over the base]
1963	  \label{lem:gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}
1964	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase}
1965	  \uses{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}
1966	  Let \(R \to B \to N\) be a scalar tower in which \(N\) is a \(B\)-module, and let
1967	  \(T \subseteq B\) be a submonoid of the \emph{source} ring \(B\). View \(N\) as an
1968	  \(R\)-module through the tower. If \(N\) is flat over \(R\), then the localized module
1969	  \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N\) is again flat over \(R\). \emph{(Note: the existing
1970	  Mathlib results \cref{lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves} localize over a
1971	  submonoid of the \emph{base} \(R\); here the submonoid lives in the source \(B\) while
1972	  the base \(R\) is untouched, so a separate argument is required.)}
1973	\end{lemma}
1974	
1975	\begin{proof}
1976	  \uses{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm, lem:mathlib_flat_localization_preserves}
1977	  Localization at \(T\) commutes with tensoring against an \(R\)-module: by
1978	  \cref{lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm}, for every \(R\)-module \(K\)
1979	  there is a natural isomorphism
1980	  \[
1981	    \mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,(N \otimes_R K)
1982	      \;\cong\; (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K,
1983	  \]
1984	  natural in \(K\) (compatible with the maps induced by any \(R\)-linear \(K \to K'\)).
1985	  Flatness of \(N\) over \(R\) means that for every injection \(K \hookrightarrow K'\) of
1986	  \(R\)-modules the induced map \(N \otimes_R K \to N \otimes_R K'\) is injective.
1987	  Localization at the submonoid \(T\) is an exact functor, so it preserves this
1988	  injectivity; transporting along the natural isomorphism turns the result into
1989	  injectivity of
1990	  \((\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K \to (\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N) \otimes_R K'\).
1991	  As this holds for every injection of \(R\)-modules, \(\mathrm{LocalizedModule}\,T\,N\)
1992	  is flat over \(R\).
1993	\end{proof}

2007	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2008	[B2.1 — cross-chart basic-open identity]
2009	  \label{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}
2010	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}
2011	  \uses{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}
2012	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(W, W_j \subseteq X\) two opens with overlap
2013	  \(O = W \sqcap W_j\), and let \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\), \(\bar g \in \Gamma(X, W_j)\) be sections
2014	  that restrict to the \emph{same} section over \(O\), i.e.\ \(g|_O = \bar g|_O\) in
2015	  \(\Gamma(X, O)\). Suppose both basic opens lie in the overlap,
2016	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \le O\) and \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le O\). Then they agree as
2017	  opens of \(X\):
2018	  \[
2019	    X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \;=\; X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g .
2020	  \]
2021	  In particular the open \(D(g)\) is computed identically whether read in the chart \(W\) or in
2022	  the chart \(W_j\), and \(D(g) = D(\bar g) \le W_j\) (no module content is involved).
2023	\end{lemma}
2024	
2025	\begin{proof}
2026	  \uses{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}
2027	  Basic opens are compatible with restriction (\cref{lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res}). For the
2028	  inclusion \(O \le W\), \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(g|_O) = O \sqcap X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g\), and
2029	  since \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g \le O\) the meet collapses to
2030	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(g|_O) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g\). Symmetrically, for \(O \le W_j\),
2031	  \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,(\bar g|_O) = O \sqcap X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g
2032	  = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\). The hypothesis \(g|_O = \bar g|_O\) makes the two left-hand
2033	  sides equal, so \(X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\).
2034	\end{proof}

2043	  \(W \le p^{-1}(U)\), a patch \(W_j = \Spec B_j\) with \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and a matched
2044	  pair \(g \in \Gamma(X, W)\), \(\bar g \in B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\) cutting out the same basic open
2045	  \(D := X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le W \sqcap W_j\) (as produced by
2046	  \cref{lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq}). Then the single group \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) carries two
2047	  localized-module structures:
2048	  \begin{enumerate}
2049	    \item the section restriction \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) as
2050	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W)\); and
2051	    \item the section restriction \(M_j \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) as
2052	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g)^{-1}\,M_j = (M_j)_{\bar g}\).
2053	  \end{enumerate}

2060	        lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}

2062	  \(D = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g\), so \(\Gamma(\F, D)\) is one and the
2063	  same group whether \(D\) is read in chart \(W\) or in chart \(W_j\). Applying the quasi-coherent
2064	  section-localization datum \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} with ambient affine \(W\)
2065	  and element \(g\), the restriction \(\Gamma(\F, W) \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) is the localization at
2066	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,g\), giving (1). Applying the same datum with ambient affine \(W_j\) and element
2067	  \(\bar g\) (legitimate since \(D = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,\bar g \le W_j\)), the restriction
2068	  \(M_j \to \Gamma(\F, D)\) is the localization at \(\mathrm{powers}\,\bar g\), giving (2). Both maps
2069	  localize their respective sources onto the same target \(\Gamma(\F, D)\); the universal property of
2070	  localized modules (\(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\) uniqueness) supplies the canonical isomorphism
2071	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,g)^{-1}\,\Gamma(\F, W) \cong (M_j)_{\bar g}\). Finiteness of \(M_j\) over \(B_j\)
2072	  is recorded by \cref{lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections}.
2073	\end{proof}

2100	  \(A_f \to R\) is the restriction along \(U \le D(f)\) and exhibits \(R\) as a localization of

2104	\subsection{Base descent along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow V\)}
2105	\label{sec:gf_base_descent_isEpi}
2106	
2107	The generic-flatness construction yields, on each patch, flatness of the section module
2108	over the base ring \(\Gamma(S, V) = A_f\) of the witness open \(V = D(f)\). For an
2109	\emph{arbitrary} affine open \(U \le V\), the base ring \(R = \Gamma(S, U)\) need not be a
2110	localization of \(A_f\), so the localization-descent of
2111	\cref{lem:gf_base_localization_comparison} does not transport flatness directly. The correct
2112	mechanism is that the inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow V\) of affine opens is an \emph{open
2113	immersion}, hence a monomorphism of schemes, so the restriction ring map
2114	\(\Gamma(S, V) \to \Gamma(S, U)\) is a ring \emph{epimorphism}. For a ring epimorphism
2115	\(A \to R\) the canonical base-change map \(R \otimes_A M \to M\) is an isomorphism, and
2116	flatness descends along it. This replaces the earlier \texttt{IsBaseChange} hypothesis (once
2117	flagged as absent from Mathlib) by the verified Mathlib epimorphism API.
2118	
2119	\begin{definition}[Mathlib anchor — ring-epimorphism typeclass]
2120	  \label{lem:mathlib_algebra_isEpi}
2121	  \lean{Algebra.IsEpi}
2122	  \mathlibok
2123	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Epi}).}
2124	  For a commutative ring \(R\) and an \(R\)-algebra \(A\), the predicate
2125	  \(\mathrm{Algebra.IsEpi}\,R\,A\) asserts that the structure map \(R \to A\) is an
2126	  epimorphism in the category of commutative rings (equivalently, the multiplication map
2127	  \(A \otimes_R A \to A\) is an isomorphism).
2128	\end{definition}
2129

2138	  the canonical map exhibits the base change \(A \otimes_R M\) as \(M\) itself, an

2331	  \(W\) with \(x \in D(g) \subseteq D(b)\) (in chart \(W\)); hence \(D(g) \le W \sqcap W_i\).

2401	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
2402	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
2403	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
2404	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
2405	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.

2408	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).

2430	  \(U_0\) is affine, hence quasi-compact, the preimage \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\) is
2431	  quasi-compact; choose a
2432	  finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of
2433	  \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\)
2434	  is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.

2437	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the

2445	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
2446	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and

3444	      --   • `Γ(F, D ḡ)` is the source-localization of `M_i` at `powers ḡ`
3445	      --     (`Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F (i affine) gbar`), and `f` is a
3446	      --     unit on `D ḡ ⊆ p⁻¹U ⊆ p⁻¹V`, so `Γ(F, D ḡ) = ((M_i)_f)_ḡ`; B1

3448	      --     untouched) keeps `Af`-flatness under the source localization at `powers ḡ`.

3451	      -- lemmas; it is the ONLY open step of `genericFlatness` and carries no Mathlib gap.
3452	      -- ── Patch data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

3454	      have hWi : IsAffineOpen Wi := (i : ↑X.affineOpens).2

3456	      letI algA : Algebra ↑A Γ(X, Wi) := (p.appLE U₀ Wi hWile).hom.toAlgebra
3457	      letI modAMi : Module ↑A Γ(F, Wi) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wi hWile).hom
3458	      haveI towerAMi : IsScalarTower ↑A Γ(X, Wi) Γ(F, Wi) :=
3459	        IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
3460	      -- `f̄ = image of f in `Γ(X, Wi)`; `D f̄ = Wi ⊓ p⁻¹(D f)` lies over `D f`.

3463	      set Dfbar : X.Opens := X.basicOpen fbar with hDfbar_def

3474	        rw [hfdef]; exact (Finset.mul_prod_erase sf f0 hi_sf).symm

1940	end GenericFreeness
1941	
1942	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1943	
1944	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1945	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1946	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1947	
1948	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
1949	
1950	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1951	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1952	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1953	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1954	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1955	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1956	
1957	* **Surviving residue** (`sorry` this iter): when `M` is finite over the
1958	  *finite-type* algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A`, the genuine §4
1959	  dévissage is required — a prime filtration of `M` as a finite `B`-module
1960	  reduces to `M = B/𝔭`, Noether normalisation makes `B_g` finite over the
1961	  polynomial ring `A_g[b₁,…,b_n]`, and induction on the support dimension
1962	  bottoms out at the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness. That core
1963	  (a finite module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]` is generically free) is the precise
1964	  piece Mathlib does not yet supply. -/
1965	
1966	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1967	-- The §4 dévissage assembly in the `B/𝔭` branch (Noether normalisation, the
1968	-- polynomial-ring core, and the ring↔module localisation bridge) drives the deep
1969	-- localisation/quotient instance stack past the default elaboration and
1970	-- instance-search heartbeat limits; both are raised for this declaration.
1971	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
1972	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1973	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1974	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1975	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1976	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1977	
1978	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1979	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1980	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (Noether normalisation +

### `AlgebraicGeometry.flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` (line 2827)
- **Blueprint coverage**: none
- **What it says**: Flatness transfers across a ring isomorphism `e : R ≃+* R'` and a compatible additive isomorphism `l : M ≃+ M'` (semilinear: `l(r • x) = e(r) • l(x)`). Proof reduces to `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`.
- **Relationship to blueprint**: `lem:module_free_of_ringEquiv` (blueprint line 1063) pins the analogous *freeness* transfer `Module.Free.of_ringEquiv` as a Mathlib lemma. The flatness version is project-local and used by both `flat_localization_models` and the STEP-3 sorry close.

### `AlgebraicGeometry.flat_localization_models` (line 2886)
- **Blueprint coverage**: none
- **What it says**: If `Mₛ` and `Mₛ'` are two models of the `A`-module `M` localized at `S` (over respective localization rings `Rₛ`, `Rₛ'`), then `Mₛ` flat over `Rₛ` implies `Mₛ'` flat over `Rₛ'`. Proof: `IsLocalization.algEquiv` + `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv` are semilinear; `flat_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` applies.

The blueprint's STEP 4 prose mentions this bridge implicitly ("yields exactly `Γ(F, D(g))` flat over `A_f`") but does not name a lemma for the model-independence of flatness under localization.

### `AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars` (line 2928)
- **Blueprint coverage**: none
- **What it says**: If `φ : M →ₗ[B] N` exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `powers (algebraMap A B f)` (a submonoid of `B`), then `φ.restrictScalars A` exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` (as an `A`-module) at `powers f`. Technical descent of the `IsLocalizedModule` property along the scalar tower.
